entries.summary: Wagner private group described as an extension of Russia's military in Ukraine.
Prigozhin acknowledged founding Wagner in 2014 amid recruitment of Russian prisoners.
Wagner boss said Russian troops abandoned positions near Bakhmut in eastern Ukraine.
Al Jazeera mapped Wagner's rise from shadowy mercenary outfit to global force.
Prigozhin released audio declaring a march for justice against Russian military leadership.
Russia's FSB opened criminal case against Prigozhin for calling for armed mutiny.
Wagner fighters seized army headquarters in Rostov-on-Don without firing a shot.
Putin gave televised address accusing Prigozhin of treason and stab in the back.
Belarusian leader Lukashenko brokered deal halting Wagner's march on Moscow.
Analysts assessed Belarus's role as Lukashenko exchanged shelter for political leverage.
Wagner's Africa operations faced uncertainty as ripple effects from the mutiny spread.
Lukashenko said Prigozhin had returned to Russia after a brief exile in Belarus.
NATO chief Stoltenberg said the alliance was tracking Wagner mercenary movements.
Putin met with Prigozhin in the Kremlin as army chief Gerasimov reappeared publicly.
Private plane crashed northwest of Moscow with Prigozhin reportedly on board.
Russia confirmed Prigozhin's death via genetic testing after the deadly plane crash.
Former Wagner fighter Marat Gabidullin detailed group's struggles in Ukraine to CNN.
Investigation revealed Wagner's brutal human-wave tactics against Ukrainian positions in Donbas.
US imposed fresh sanctions on Wagner Group, designating it a transnational criminal organisation.
Wagner used Russian convict-recruits as expendable assault troops in eastern Ukraine.
Prigozhin posted videos from Bakhmut accusing Russian command of starving Wagner of ammunition.
Prigozhin threatened to withdraw Wagner from Bakhmut over the alleged ammunition shortages.
Russia's defence ministry accused Prigozhin of armed mutiny against the Russian state.
Wagner forces advanced on Moscow before Prigozhin halted convoys 200 kilometres from capital.
Western officials assessed the mutiny as the gravest challenge to Putin's two-decade rule.
Lukashenko told reporters Prigozhin had arrived in Belarus under the brokered deal.
Belarus invited Wagner to train its military as fighters relocated from Russia.
Prigozhin was photographed at the Russia-Africa summit in St Petersburg.
Prigozhin praised the military coup that ousted Niger's elected president Mohamed Bazoum.
Reports said Prigozhin was moving between Russia, Belarus, and Africa weeks after mutiny.
Prigozhin posted apparent video from desert location resembling parts of West Africa.
Plane carrying Prigozhin and Wagner founders crashed in Tver region, killing all ten.
Wagner forces remained active in Central African Republic two months after Prigozhin's death.
